content:
Character: Alice Whitfield
Perception: Alice looks like she was assembled from a catalog of good decisions — polished, poised, every hair accounted for. But her eyes give her away; there's something restless underneath all that composure. She's brilliant and kind in a way she doesn't advertise. We bonded over Woolf at the coffee shop and now she's one of the few people I genuinely look forward to seeing. I think she sees me as a quiet friend who understands the weight of unspoken things. I know she's questioning law as a path. I know about her grandmother. I've noticed the way she listens when Leo talks — careful, a little too careful.
summary:
Character: Alice Whitfield
Perception: Alice looks like she was assembled from a catalog of good decisions — polished, poised, every hair accounted for. But her eyes give her away; there's something restless underneath all that composure. She's brilliant and kind in a way she doesn't advertise. We bonded over Woolf at the coffee shop and now she's one of the few people I genuinely look forward to seeing. I think she sees me as a quiet friend who understands the weight of unspoken things. I know she's questioning law as a path. I know about her grandmother. I've noticed the way she listens when Leo talks — careful, a little too careful.
